title: Standardize performance testing methodology
body:
_Current benchmark results can be seen here: https://github.com/dapr/dapr/tree/master/tests/perf/report/charts_

## Idea

Dapr's performance test suite has grown organically across APIs, resulting in a mix of tooling and load profiles. Bringing these into alignment would make results significantly easier to compare, interpret, and present to the community.

 - We should standardize all API performance tests on a single tooling stack
   - Fortio seems like it might be the best bet as we're already using it, and it natively supports both gRPC and HTTP
 - Define a consistent load profile (QPS, connection count, operation count) to use across all API tests

## Expected Outcome

 - Comparable metrics across all Dapr APIs
 - Aligned percentile reporting (p50, p90, p99, p99.9) across every test

## Reference

If you're like me, you might not keep all the performance testing lingo committed to memory 😅, here's an AI-summary:

 - p50 — 50% of requests completed within this time. Essentially the median. Represents your "typical" experience.
 - p90 — 90% of requests completed within this time. Starts showing how the system behaves under mild stress.
 - p99 — 99% of requests completed within this time. Represents your worst 1 in 100 requests.
 - p99.9 — 99.9% of requests completed within this time. Worst 1 in 1,000 requests.
 - p99.99 (sometimes called p999 colloquially) — Worst 1 in 10,000 requests. Really only meaningful at very high sample sizes.
